example_profile_summary: 35, retirement in ~30 years, 10k lump sum + 500/mo, risk 7/10

recommendation.explanation: To help you work toward your retirement goal over the next 30 years, we have designed a portfolio focused on long-term growth. This portfolio consists entirely of stock ETFs [1, 2, 3], which are designed to participate in company profits and offer the highest potential returns over long periods, though their value may fluctuate in the short term [1, 2, 3]. By investing in a mix of the iShares Core S&P 500 ETF [1], the Vanguard Value ETF [2], and the Vanguard FTSE Developed Markets ETF [3], you gain broad exposure to established companies across the U.S. and international markets. All three funds are selected for their very low costs, which helps keep more of the market returns in your account [1, 2, 3]. To get your strategy moving, we suggest using your 10,000 EUR as a starting anchor, complemented by your monthly contribution of 500 EUR. If you feel this approach is too aggressive or conservative for your comfort, please let us know and we can easily adjust your risk level.

sources.text:
# iShares Core S&P 500 ETF (IVV)

Data as of 2026-07-08. Sources: fund metadata from the project universe, live fundamentals and 5-year price history via Yahoo Finance.

## What this fund is

This is an equity (stock) ETF. Equity funds participate in company profits and historically offer the highest long-term returns, but their value can fluctuate strongly in the short term.

iShares Core S&P 500 ETF trades under the ticker IVV in the category 'Large Blend'.
Fund size: 888.1 billion USD.

## Costs

The total expense ratio (TER) is 0.03% per year. Costs are deducted from the fund's assets automatically; lower cost means more of the market return stays with the investor.

## Risk profile (computed from 5-year price history)

- Annualized volatility: 17.0%
- Annualized return: 13.0%
- Maximum drawdown: -24.5%
- Sharpe ratio: 0.80

Fund size: 254.5 billion USD.

## Costs
